zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Champernoun於大約1227年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親Oliver de Champernon57歲，母親Eva Andea Wymarca de Cardigan53歲。她於1256年之前與William IV De Ferrers在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1269年在英國英格蘭戴文貝 費雷斯享年44歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Champernoun was born about 1227, in Devon, England, her father, Oliver de Champernon, was 57 and her mother, Eva Andea Wymarca de Cardigan, was 53. She married William IV De Ferrers before 1256, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died about 1269, in Bere Ferrers, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Champernoun est née environ 1227, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Oliver de Champernon, avait 57 ans et sa mère, Eva Andea Wymarca de Cardigan, avait 53 ans. Elle a épousé William IV De Ferrers avant 1256, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1269, à Bere Ferrers, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans.

ru.Clyst Champernon, Devonshire, England: клист ст джеордж, девон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Champernoun родилась около 1227 г. в местности девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Oliver de Champernon было 57 лет, а матери, которую звали Eva Andea Wymarca de Cardigan, 53. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William IV De Ferrers перед 1256 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1269 г. в местности бир фиррирс, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет.
